title: RPYR-0002 — `_asyncio.current_task()` aborts when `_current_tasks` is rebound to a non-dict: `downcast::<PyDict>().unwrap()` (`_asyncio.rs:2408`)
cpython_behavior: reads its own thread-state task registry; a reassigned _asyncio._current_tasks does not crash the interpreter
guarded_twin: crates/stdlib/src/_asyncio.rs — _enter_task (2503), _leave_task, _swap_current_task all downcast the SAME _current_tasks dict with `if let Ok(dict) = current_tasks.downcast::<PyDict>()`; only current_task uses .unwrap()
notes: `_current_tasks` is a plain, Python-writable module attribute seeded as a PyDict via extend_module!, so a Python program can rebind it. get_current_tasks_dict reads it via vm.get_attribute_opt; the slow path (is_current_loop == false, e.g. an explicit loop= with no running loop) then downcasts+unwraps. Both the panic auditor (downcast_or_coerce signal) and the history agent (similar-bug: the three guarded twins vs the one bare unwrap) landed on this independently.
